text: Dr Ashleigh Witt 2013 Chest Pain Don’t miss: MI, Dissection, PE, Pneumothorax Vitals, ECG, IV access x2, CXR, Serial troponins, check LL for DVT, BP both arms Mx: Morphine, Oxygen, Nitrates, Aspirin Abdominal Pain Don’t miss: AAA, Bowel isch/perf, cholang/append/cholecystitis FBE, CMP, Lipase, Lactate, bHCG, AXR, CXR, ECG, LFTs Mx: Surg r/v, imaging, NBM, IV fluids, morphine Altered Mental State Don’t miss: Meningitis, sepsis, CVA, ICP FBE, CMP, TSH, UEC, Blood & Urine Cx, ECG, CXR Consider: LP, CTB, EEG Med r/v Narcotics, H2 blockers, TCAs, benzos Acute Renal Failure Don’t miss Hyperkalaemia, prostate Urinalys, UEC, CMP, FBE, Bladder scan, ?ECG/Renal US/ABG Put foley in/flush foley/change foley Med r/v: ACEI, ARB, NSAIDs, Diuretics, ABx, contrast Shortness of Breath Don’t miss: PE, MI, Tamponade, Hypoxia, CCF, pneumonia O2 Sats, Auscultate, ECG, ABG, CXR, D-Dimer, CRP PE: S1 Q3 T3 CCF Mx: Lasix 40mg-80mg iv Asthma: Ventolin & Atrovent nebs Headache Don’t miss: Meningitis, Subarach, Subdural FBE, CRP, Fundoscopy, Neuro exam, ?LP, ?Image Mx: Paracetamol, NSAIDs if young, Opiods Hypoglycaemia <6: check mental state, juice or sweet beverage <4 or altered mental state: 50% dextrose iv, IM glucagon if no access Hyperglycaemia Commence tds top up scale with meals BSL Usual daily insulin <50 u Usual daily insulin >50 u Elderly 10.1- 14 4 u novorapid 6 u novorapid - 14.1- 18 6 u novorapid 8 u novorapid 4 u novorapid >18.1 8 u novorapid 10 u novorapid 6 u novorapid Hypotension Don’t miss: Shock (SBP<90 w poor perfusion, increased HR) Obs Monitoring, ECG, IV access, FBE, CMP, CXR, Cultures, Troponin, Full exam Mx: Fluid bolus Hypertension Aim for 15%  in MABP over 2-3h to avoid stroke Mx: Severe, symptomatic – GTN patch & neuro r/v Asympt, chronic HTN <200/120 consider ACEI or BB Fever Don’t miss: Sepsis, Meningitis FBE, Blood Cx, CRP, CXR, Urine MCS, Sputum Culture, Check lines & catheter, Check wound, surg site, Consider LP Mx: Await cultures, if uncomfortable paracetamol 1g Diarrhoea Don’t miss: C. Difficile, too many aperients, overflow Mx: Loperamide 4mg PO, then 2mg after each loose stool Oral rehydration or IV fluids Constipation Don’t miss: Bowel Obs PR, Abdo XR Mx: Movicol 1-2 sachets m, Coloxyl Senna 2 bd, Fleet enema x 1, Lactulose, glyceryl suppose PR PRN Nausea/Vomiting Mx: Metoclopramide, chlorpromazine, ondanestron, domperidone Hypokalaemia Check CMP, KCL is hard to correct if Mg is low Mx: 2 x chlorvescent or 2 x slow K or 10mmol KCL in 100mL N/S or 30mmol KCL in 1L Hyperkalaemia If ECG shows peaked T-waves, wide QRS – telemetry, registrar + calcium gluconate GI Bleed Don’t miss: shock Group & hold, Coags, CMP, FBE, UEC Mx: IVs x 2, vit K, FFP, NS, GI consult Seizures Remove danger, if >5min PR diazepam 30mg or iv lorazepam 0.2mg/kg Pain Killers Paracetamol: 1g q6h (max 4g/24h) Oxycontin: 10-30mg bd Endone: 5-15mg q4-6h Anti-emetics Metoclopramide: 10mg PO/IM/IV q4-6h Ondanestron 4mg PO or Subling bd Domperidone 10-20mg PO qid Laxatives Docusate&Senna 50mg/8mg 2 tab bd Lactulose 15-30mL d in 1-2 doses Fleet enema 1 stat. Cardiac Drugs GTN 300-600mcg (0.5-1 tab SL) GTN patch 10mg/24h Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5-25mg/25-100mg (Ht/Fluid) Perindopril 2.5mg/Ramipril 2.5-5mg/Catopril 12.5mg bd Candesartan 8-16mg/Telmisartan 20-40/Irbesartan 150-300mg Amlodipidine 2.5-10mg Atenolol 25-100mg/Metoprolol 50-100mg/Propranolol20-320mg Antibiotics Amoxyl+Clav 500+125mg bd 5d Gentamycin 5mg/kg up to 480mg Ceftriaxone 1g iv d Azithromycin 1g PO Benzylpenicillin 1.2g IV 6h Metronidazole 400mg PO 12h Trimethoprim 300mg PO 3d Doxycycline 100mg PO 12h 7d Other Risperidone 1mg PO up to 6mg Haloperidol 0.25mg – 0.5mg s/cut, po, IM Esomeprazole 20mg PO 1d Temazepam 10mg PO Benztropine 1-2mg IM or 1-2mg Dr Ashleigh Witt 2013
